title: List of Legends elements in the films
content:
{{Top|real}}
{{Citation}}
'''C-[[canon]] elements from licensed [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]]''' (EU) creators have appeared '''in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' films''', mainly the [[prequel trilogy]]. Most of these are brief appearances, almost taking the form of [[Easter egg (virtual)|Easter eggs]], and are even hardly distinguishable. However, others are more substantial.

The prequel trilogy also has many [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO#Prequel trilogy references|references]] to names and elements introduced in the television series [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars Droids'']] due to [[Ben Burtt]]'s large involvement in both, not the least of which is the cameo by himself as [[Ebenn Q3 Baobab/Legends|Ebenn Q3 Baobab]] in ''The Phantom Menace''.

The reverse process is also true: many Expanded Universe works have given names and/or background information to nearly every element shown in the films. Additionally, the [[Star Wars Visual Dictionaries|''Visual Dictionary'']] series, along with other official sources such as "[[What's The Story?]]," has also introduced many [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]]s, thus "creating" C-canon depictions in films—e.g. the ''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary]]'' identifies [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku's]] [[Vjun tunic|tunic]] in ''Revenge of the Sith'' as being created on [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] found heretofore strictly in the EU. The significance of these identifications, for the most part, is downplayed in this article.

After the reboot of [[2014]], EU material has been harvested for use in the new continuity, and films including [[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens|''Star Wars'': Episode VII ''The Force Awakens'']] borrow Legends material.

==[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]==
[[File:EbennStandingAround-TPM.jpg|thumb|right|100px|Baobab at the celebrations following the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]]]
*The species name [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] first appeared in ''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]'', a [[West End Games]] sourcebook, but was used in the [[prequel trilogy]] in [[George Lucas]]'s handwritten scripts and production notes (although it is never mentioned in dialogue).
*The name "[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]" was coined by [[Timothy Zahn]] for [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|''The Thrawn Trilogy'']]. Lucas developed the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] of the capital called [[Had Abbadon/Legends|Had Abbadon]] and planned to include the planet in ''Return of the Jedi'', but decided to adopt the name created by Zahn for the Imperial capital when presenting the planet in the [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|Special Editions]] and prequel trilogy.<ref>{{SWArchive|url=community/askjc/steve/askjc20030611.html|text=Star Wars: Community – Who created Coruscant}}</ref>
*The writing system called "[[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]]" that appears is the one created for ''[[Star Wars Miniatures Battles Companion]]'', a West End Games rulebook, by [[Stephen Crane]], who had, in turn based it on meaningless script appearing in the [[original trilogy]].<ref name="Aurebesh Soup">{{WebCite|url=http://www.echostation.com/features/aurebesh.htm|text=Aurebesh Soup: Recipe for a Star Wars Font Phenomenon|author=[[Stephen Crane|Crane, Stephen]]|date=2000-10-21|work=echostation.com|archivedate=20160520153232|nolive=1}}</ref>
*[[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] being a species, rather than a gangster title (as originally suggested), originated in ''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]'', a West End Games sourcebook.
*The [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]] is mentioned, having first appeared in the [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|first edition of West End Games' ''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']], based on the concept of the Jedi Oath developed in the early drafts for ''Star Wars Episode V:'' ''The'' ''Empire Strikes Back.''<ref>[[Star Wars: The Annotated Screenplays]]</ref>
*[[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] resembles the [[Boonta Speeder Race]] from the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Droids'']] episode "[[A Race to the Finish]]."
*The physics of how podracers fly was inspired in part by what the LucasArts team created for ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]].''<ref name="Forcematerial" />

*'''The following could have been intentional EU references, or later retcons, coincidences, or EU extrapolations:'''
**[[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] appears as a spectator at the [[Mos Espa Grand Arena]]. A [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Micro Machines|Micro-Machines]] model of the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|''Shadows of the Empire'']] villain was used to populate the crowd, possibly randomly, without canonical significance.
**[[Toong/Legends|Toong]], the species of [[Ben Quadinaros/Legends|Ben Quadinaros]], was mentioned in ''Droids'', while his homeworld, [[Tund/Legends|Tund]], was first mentioned in ''[[The Lando Calrissian Adventures]]'' and appeared in that trilogy's final volume, ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]''.
**The [[Theelin/Legends|Theelin]] species appears, in the form of [[Diva Shaliqua/Legends|Diva Shaliqua]]. The species first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]''. (Another Theelin had already appeared in the ''Return of the Jedi'' Special Edition; see below.)
**[[Ebenn Q3 Baobab/Legends|Ebenn Q3 Baobab]] may be a member of the [[Baobab family]] introduced in ''Droids''. The role was a cameo by sound designer [[Ben Burtt]] who intentionally chose the name to link his character to the show.
**Shmi and the others watch the race through a device described as a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]]. Datapads as "data pads" first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and adapted into ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' and have shown up in other EU books since.
**[[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul's]] [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]] was a design originally seen wielded by [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|third]] and [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 5|fifth]] issues of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War|''Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War'']] comic book series.

==[[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]==
[[File:SW1998-33-Textless.jpg|thumb|right|100px|The painting by [[Jon Foster]] that secured Aayla's place in the ''Star Wars'' saga]]
*[[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]] appeared as a minor character in the movies, played by [[Amy Allen]]. The character was introduced in [[Star Wars: Twilight|''Star Wars: Republic: Twilight'']], released after ''The Phantom Menace''. George Lucas liked her illustration in a cover art and decided to adapt her character.
*[[Action VI bulk transport/Legends|Action VI transports]] initially appeared as the ''[[Wild Karrde]]'' in ''[[Heir to the Empire]]''. They also arrive at the [[Theed Spaceport/Legends|Theed Spaceport]] in ''Attack of the Clones''.
*The planet [[Rishi/Legends|Rishi]], introduced in ''[[Dark Force Rising]]'', was given a mention via the [[Rishi Maze/Legends|Rishi Maze]] in ''Attack of the Clones''.
*The swamp planet of [[Bogden/Legends|Bogden]] that was mentioned by [[Jango Fett]] appeared in the ''Droids'' episode "[[The Revenge of Kybo Ren]]."
*When Obi-Wan uses a lightsaber blade to block Dooku's [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], this is possibly inspired by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] doing the same to [[Joruus C'baoth]]'s lightning in the second novel of the [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|Thrawn trilogy]], ''[[Dark Force Rising]]''. This was visually depicted in the [[Star Wars: The Last Command (TPB)|comic adaptation]], from which George Lucas also chose to highlight some panels in the book containing his favorite comic artwork, ''[[Star Wars Art: Comics]]''. Author [[Timothy Zahn]] has stated he believes that George took inspiration of this ability from this comic depiction for the film.<ref name=":0">{{WebCite|url=http://insidemovies.ew.com/2012/11/02/star-wars-sequels-timothy-zahn|text='Star Wars' sequel author Timothy Zahn weighs in on new movie plans -- EXCLUSIVE|author=Anthony Breznican|date=2012-11-02|work=Entertainment Weekly|quote="Another thing in the Thrawn trilogy was the catching of Force lightning with a lightsaber blade. Apparently, George thought that was a cool visual and put it in Attack of the Clones and Revenge of the Sith, so he has been keeping a close eye on what has been done." -- Timothy Zahn|archivedate=20130103094610}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{WebCite|work:=SyFy|url=https://www.syfy.com/syfywire/author-timothy-zahn-starcraft-rogue-one-and-why-george-lucas-doesnt-read-novels|text=Author Timothy Zahn on Starcraft, Rogue One, and Why George Lucas Doesn't Read Novels|quote="I don’t think he ever read the novels. But my trilogy was adapted into comics, and I’d heard he does read comics. It’s a visual medium. He’s a visual medium guy, movies and such. I can only assume that’s where he picked up the catching the force lightning on a lightsaber blade [in Revenge of the Sith]. Because I’d written that into Dark Force Rising. And I’m sure he pulled it from that, but probably from the Dark Horse adaptation. So I thought it was a cool idea and it would make a cool visual, and by golly it does." -- Timothy Zahn|archivedate=20210516115839}}</ref>
*[[Luminara Unduli]] and [[Barriss Offee]], the two Jedi sent to rescue [[Anakin Skywalker]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Padmé Amidala]], albeit written for the film, first introduced to the audience in the novels ''[[Cloak of Deception]]'' by [[James Luceno]] and ''[[The Approaching Storm]]'' by [[Alan Dean Foster]].
*[[Dex's Diner/Legends|Dex's Diner]] is similar to the diner in an episode of ''Droids''.
*The concept of [[Power coupling/Legends|power couplings]] (of the type that Obi-Wan and Anakin flew through) first appeared in the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' comics.
*The depiction of the nightlife of Coruscant was inspired in part by illustrations [[Ralph McQuarrie]] made, as well as descriptions [[Kevin J. Anderson]] wrote, for ''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''.
[[File:JediKnightIICoruscant.png|thumb|right|The apocryphal ending of ''Jedi Knight'']]

*'''The following may have been intentional EU references, or later retcons, coincidences, or EU extrapolations:'''
**In the Expanded Universe material, [[San Hill/Legends|San Hill]] is identified as a [[Muun/Legends|Muun]] from [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]], a planet first mentioned in ''[[Specter of the Past]]''.
**Footage similar to that found in [[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']] can be seen on a screen in the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]].
**The [[The Works/Legends|region of Coruscant]] where Dooku arrives and meets Sidious resembles the Coruscant scenes in the alternate dark-side ending of ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''. However, in ''Jedi Knight'', the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] appears instead of the [[LiMerge Building/Legends|LiMerge Building]].
**[[Repulsorlift chair/Legends|Power chairs]] first appeared in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith|''Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith'']]. However, the concept of an antigravity seat is common in science fiction, so this may be mere coincidence.
**[[Mace Windu]] wields a purple bladed lightsaber. The concept of a purple saber first appeared in the ''[[Star Wars: The Jedi Academy Trilogy|Jedi Academy]]'' trilogy, but was first depicted visually in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Masters of Teräs Käsi]]'' utilised by [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]],<ref name="Masters Blog">''[[Star Wars: Masters of Teräs Käsi]]''</ref> although in Windu's case, the color was a personal request of [[Samuel L. Jackson]]. Before that, George Lucas insisted that Jedi lightsabers are blue or green.<ref name="Graham Norton Show">{{OfficialYouTube|channel=@BBC|name=BBC|video=ubyo5eZz_Jc|text=Why Samuel L. Jackson has a purple light sabre | The Graham Norton Show – BBC}}</ref>
**[[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] wields a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] with a [[Curved-hilt lightsaber/Legends|curved hilt]]. In the short story "[[Bane of the Sith]]." written by Kevin J. Anderson Darth Bane uses a "hooked handled" saber, although the curved hilt in the film designed by [[Dermot Power]], who wanted to achieve an "exotic, almost arabic" look.<ref>[[The Art of Star Wars Episode II: Attack of the Clones|The Art of Star Wars: Attack of the Clones]]</ref>

==[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]==
[[File:DookuForceChokeKenobi-RotS.jpg|thumb|right|A scene familiar to gamers appears for the first time in a film]]
''Revenge of the Sith'' has several elements that first appeared in Legends sources, such as ''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]'', that were written during the movie's production. However, since those sources borrowed those elements directly from the finished post-production movie script (for example, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] was written for the film but first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]''), they are not included.
*The form of [[Telekinesis#Force Grip|Force Grip]] that appears in the scene with Darth Tyranus and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan]] (immobilizing and lifting the target) first appeared in the ''Jedi Knight'' games.
*[[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon's]] [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7 ''Aethersprite''-class light interceptor]] has the same paint scheme as the ''[[Azure Angel]]''.
*Legends character [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] is mentioned (as "Master Vos") by Obi-Wan Kenobi in the one of the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple's]] many [[Jedi Temple communication center/Legends|data rooms]]. (Vos in turn was based on a background extra at the [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] café in ''The Phantom Menace''.) A cameo of this character during the execution of [[Order 66]] was planned but not filmed.
*As first mentioned in ''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]'', [[Aldera/Legends|Aldera]] is depicted as at the very least being close to a lake, if not at the center as previously noted.
*Sound clips of [[Attichitcuk/Legends|Attichitcuk's]] growls from ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'' were used for a few of [[Tarfful/Legends|Tarfful's]] growls during [[Yoda]]'s goodbye scene to him and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]].<ref>''Revenge of the Sith'' on-line DVD text commentary</ref> Additionally, ''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith Incredible Cross-Sections]]'' shows Attichitcuk using one of the gun platforms during the Battle of Kashyyyk.
*The sinkhole grottoes of [[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]] resemble the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] caves on [[Tarnoonga/Legends|Tarnoonga]] as seen in the ''Droids'' episode "[[The Pirates of Tarnoonga]]" and, to a lesser extent, the [[Great Well]] on [[Ophideraan]], from the "[[The Serpent Masters]]" story arc of the [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' newspaper comic strip]].
*General Grievous's [[Tsmeu-6 personal wheel bike|wheel bike]] is similar to [[Jann Tosh]]'s vehicle in ''Droids''.<ref>{{DB|vehicle|wheelbike|Wheel bike}}</ref>
*[[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]] appears in the beginning of the Kashyyyk scene, riding a [[Can-cell/Legends|can-cell]].
*When Mace Windu and Yoda use a lightsaber blade to block Palpatine's [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], this is possibly inspired by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] doing the same to [[Joruus C'baoth]]'s lightning in the third novel of the [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|Thrawn trilogy]], ''[[The Last Command]]''. This was visually depicted in the [[Star Wars: The Last Command (TPB)|comic adaptation]], from which George Lucas also chose to highlight some panels in the book containing his favorite comic artwork, ''[[Star Wars Art: Comics]]''. Author [[Timothy Zahn]] has stated he believes that George took inspiration of this ability from this comic depiction for the film.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />

*'''The following may have been intentional EU references, or later retcons, coincidences, or EU extrapolations:'''
**[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker's]] double-lightsaber decapitation of Count Dooku is similar to Luke Skywalker's defeat of [[Shimrra Jamaane]] in ''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''.
**The [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] was canonized in ''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' before being included in ''Revenge of the Sith''. In turn, it was a real-life charity organization, being therefore also [[List of fanon elements in continuity|a fanon element that made its way into continuity]].
**A [[HAVw A6 Juggernaut/Legends|Juggernaut]] tank is prominently seen during the [[Battle of Kashyyyk/Legends|Battle of Kashyyyk]]. Although based on unused original [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] concept art, therefore, not a Legends content, the Juggernaut was established in the Legends continuity as a siege vehicle of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]].
**[[Notluwiski Papanoida/Legends|Baron Papanoida]] had been identified as a [[Wroonian/Legends|Wroonian]] before his species was retconned as a [[Pantoran/Legends|Pantoran]]. Wroonian appeared first in [[Star Wars (1977) 7|issue seven]] of the [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'']] comic series.
**Padmé Amidala says, "This war represents a failure to listen." The character [[Mira (Near-Human)|Mira]] says the same sentence in ''[[Jedi: Mace Windu]]''.
**The film's [[Pistoeka sabotage droid/Legends|Buzz droids]] serve a purpose very similar to the Expanded Universe's [[Grutchin]]s.[[File:Palp-frieze1.jpg|thumb|300px|An elaborated [[Great Hyperspace War Bas-Relief|frieze]] kept in [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] Palpatine's chambers, later claimed to be depicting a battle during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] by [[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]]]

*'''The following are retcons:'''
**The [[Room of a Thousand Fountains/Legends|Room of a Thousand Fountains]] appears in a hologram when [[Cin Drallig/Legends|Cin Drallig]] fights with Darth Vader; it was first mentioned in ''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Rising Force]]''.
**[[Nee Alavar/Legends|Nee Alavar]], a background character, was identified as a [[Lorrdian]]. The Lorrdian people first appeared in the novel ''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]''.
**The [[Great Hyperspace War Bas-Relief|relief]] visible in Palpatine's office in the film was identified by [[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']] as a frieze depicting the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]].<ref name="CL">[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]</ref> However, according to George Lucas, there was never a war between Jedi and Sith,<ref>[[The Star Wars Archives: Episodes I–III, 1999–2005]]</ref> furthermore, the frieze itself intended to show "Jedi and aliens and warriors fighting each other"<ref>[[The Art of Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith]]</ref> with some of the aliens resembling "grays".<ref>[[Creating the Worlds of Star Wars: 365 Days]]</ref>
**[[Salporin/Legends|Salporin]] first appeared in ''[[Heir to the Empire]]''. A [[Wookiee]] in the movie has been [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] to be Salporin.
**[[Olana Chion/Legends|Olana Chion]], a Jedi, first appeared in [[Jude Watson]]'s ''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Fight for Truth]]''.

==[[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]==
[[File:Mos_Eisley.png|thumb|right|250px|The ''Outrider'' (upper left) taking off from Mos Eisley]]
*The [[Outrider/Legends|''Outrider'']] (and with it, [[YT-2400 light freighter]]s generally) were added to a scene in the Special Edition. The craft can be seen from a distance lifting off from [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]]. This ship was first designed by [[Jon Knoles]] and [[Doug Chiang]] for ''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|Shadows of the Empire]]'', and after [[LucasArts]] started cooperating more thoroughly with [[Industrial Light & Magic|Industrial Light and Magic]], the latter were impressed with a high-resolution render Knoles had made of the ''Outrider'' for ''Shadows of the Empire''. They chose to include the craft in the film, and in marketing material it also was highlighted as being the ''Outrider'' from ''Shadows of the Empire''.<ref name="Forcematerial">{{WebCite|url=https://www.forcematerial.com/home/2020/5/4/jon-knoles-shadows-of-the-empire-episode-1-racer-lucasarts|text=Force Material Episode LXXXVII: Jon Knoles on Shadows of the Empire, Episode I Racer and the legacy of LucasArts|date=2020-05-04|format=Podcast|archivedate=20200610171907}}</ref><ref name=":2">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'', Part 4: Battle Zones, II. Battleships</ref>
*Another addition from ''Shadows of the Empire'' is the appearance of the [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|Sentinel-class landing craft]] seen in the new [[Dewback/Legends|dewback]] scene as well as above Mos Eisley. While looking through LucasArts' library of ship models, Industrial Light and Magic were impressed that the detail of LucasArts' render of the ship surpassed their own, so they included LucasArts version of the craft in the film.<ref name="Forcematerial" />
*[[Swoop]] bikes were a new addition to the film, and they were redesigned and added to ''Shadows of the Empire''. In the Special Edition scenes added to [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']], a swoop bike scares a [[Ronto/Legends|ronto]].<ref name=":2" />
*[[ASP-series labor droid/Legends|ASP-series droid]], had appeared in ''Shadows of the Empire''.
*The [[tractor beam]] characters were changed from English to [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]] for the 2004 DVD Edition. Of note is the fact that the Aurebesh had appared in original releases of the original trilogy (i.e. the clearance code screen for [[Tydirium/Legends|''Tydirium'']] in ''Return of the Jedi'') is made up of nonsense characters. West End Games created a 'translation" to English letters for [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]] based on what had appeared onscreen. When using these newly assigned equivalents, most of the original trilogy Aurebesh remains unintelligible; however, in the [[prequel trilogy]] and a few [[List of changes in Star Wars re-releases|remastered]] original trilogy examples, it has an actual meaning.

*'''It is not known if the following were intentional EU references, or were later retcons, coincidences, or EU extrapolations:'''
:*In the [[Star Wars (1991 video game)|''Star Wars'']] video game, [[Boba Fett]] is shown as being present on Mos Eisely. In the 1997 Special Edition, Boba Fett was newly added into a scene taking place in nearly the same location, confirming the character's presence on the planet at this time.

==[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]==
*[[Tibanna gas platform|Tibanna gas platforms]] were added to the Special Edition version of the film from a painting [[Ralph McQuarrie]] did in the 1990s, in coordination with [[Kevin J. Anderson]], for ''The Illustrated Star Wars Universe.'' Other buildings of [[Cloud City]], created for the Special Edition by Industrial Light and Magic, were also inspired by the paintings for the book. The Tibanna gas platforms in ''The Empire Strikes Back'' can be seen as Luke flies to [[Cloud City]].
*[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], who first appeared in the ''Holiday Special'' in 1978, plays a major role as the individual who finds the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. However, the character of Fett was designed for ''The Empire Strikes Back'' but revealed to the public through the Holiday Special, much like with Grievous some twenty-six years later.
*A similar title, [[Star Wars (1977) 18|''The Empire Strikes'']], had been used for the December 1978 issue of the Marvel ''Star Wars'' series.

==[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]==
*The [[Theelin]] species, in the person of [[Rystáll Sant]], appears in the Special Edition. The Theelin species had first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]''.
*Boba Fett's rope weapon and [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]] had originally appeared in the ''Holiday Special'', albeit the weapons were designed for ''The Empire Strikes Back''.
*A beard was added to [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine's]] costume when it was realized that Kenner was designing the toy with a beard.

==[[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens|''Star Wars'': Episode VII ''The Force Awakens'']]==
*[[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren's]] name has a one-letter difference from [[Gir Kybo Ren-Cha|Ky'''b'''o Ren]], a villain from ''Star Wars: Droids''.
*Kylo Ren is similar to [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo/Darth Caedus]], a main character in many Legends novels. Like Kylo Ren, he was the son of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].
**In Legends, Jacen Solo became the apprentice of [[Lumiya]] and joined [[Lumiya's Sith]], and in a similar manner Ben Solo became the apprentice of [[Snoke]] and joined the [[Knights of Ren]].
*After Kylo Ren's fall to the [[dark side of the Force]], Luke goes into [[Exile (punishment)|exile]]. In Legends, he was [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] too by [[Natasi Daala]] for Jacen's fall to the dark side in the novel ''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]''.
*Kylo Ren kills a family member, in his case his father, after his turn to the dark side.<ref name="Episode VII">{{Film|VII}}</ref> In Legends, Jacen killed his aunt [[Mara Jade Skywalker]] in the novel ''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''.<ref name="Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref> In the ''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'', the similarity is made explicit by the introduction of a [[Sith]] [[trial of ascendancy]] that required sacrifice of a loved one, which Kylo Ren, unbeknownst to himself fulfilled.<ref name="TROS VD">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> The trial was a part of the Prophecy of the Sith mentioned in the [[Lorrd Artifact]] in the novel ''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]''.<ref name="Bloodlines">''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]''</ref>
*Kylo Ren's birth name is "Ben Solo" after Obi-Wan Kenobi. "Ben" was the name of [[Ben Skywalker|the child]] of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] in Legends and was too named after [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]].
*[[Stormtrooper (First Order)|First Order Stormtroopers]] in Canon play a similar role to the [[Galactic Alliance Guard]] in Legends in that they serve as soldiers under the command of Kylo Ren/Darth Caedus.
*Until this film introduced Captain [[Phasma]], the only other female Stormtrooper was [[Jes Gistang]], a character from the Legends continuity.
**Phasma's character has similarities with [[Tamith Kai]] from Legends. Both characters were instructors responsible for recruiting, combat training and discipline, both were warriors originally members of a clan.
*The term "Light" (for the Light Side) is mentioned.
*Kylo Ren has a [[Kylo Ren's lightsaber|crossguard lightsaber]] as his weapon. A [[Crossguard lightsaber/Legends|crossguard lightsaber]] first appeared in the comic book [[Republic 61|''Republic'' 61]].
*Luke Skywalker also trained a [[New Jedi Order|new generation of Jedi]] in the Legends continuity, who particularly served as the main focus of the novel series ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order]]''. Unlike Legends, Luke's Jedi came to an end abruptly.
*The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] was first introduced and created for the novel ''[[Heir to the Empire]]''.
*Han Solo mentions the [[Moof]], which was first pictured in [[Vow of Justice|''Star Wars: Republic: Vow of Justice'']].
*The film's title was at one point considered to be ''Shadow of the Empire'', likely as a reference to the ''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' multimedia project, though it was ultimately dropped according to [[Pablo Hidalgo]].<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/insertcoin/2015/12/28/star-wars-the-force-awakens-was-once-called-shadow-of-the-empire|text='Star Wars: The Force Awakens' Was Once Called 'Shadow of the Empire'|author=Paul Tassi|date=2015-12-28|work=[[Wikipedia:Forbes|''Forbes'']]|archivedate=20210924061922}}</ref>

==''[[Rogue One: A Star Wars Story]]''==
*[[Galen Walton Erso]]'s first name was also the first name of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Galen Marek|secret apprentice]] in the video game ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'' and [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II|its sequel]].
*[[Jyn Erso]]'s name is similar to [[Jan Ors]], the sidekick of [[Kyle Katarn]] who [[Mission to Danuta|stole]] the [[Death Star plans/Legends|Death Star plans]] in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]''.
*Early concept art for the film used the [[Temple of Pomojema]] as a setting, but it was ultimately replaced with the [[Temple of the Kyber]].
*Darth Vader tries to heal himself in a [[bacta tank]], similar to when Vader tries to use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to heal himself in the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'' novel]].
*The film shows Darth Vader having a [[Fortress Vader|castle]] at [[Mustafar]], taking inspiration from [[Ralph McQuarrie]] concept art for ''The Empire Strikes Back''. In Legends, Vader had a [[Darth Vader's Palace|palace]] at Coruscant and a [[Bast Castle|castle]] at [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]], respectively seen in the ''Shadows of the Empire'' novel and in [[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]].
*The film's climax takes place during the [[Battle of Scarif]], where the [[Death Star plans]] are stolen. In the Legends continuity, the battle in which the [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] were stolen was identified as the [[Battle of Toprawa]] by the [[Star Wars (radio)|''Star Wars'' radio drama]], taking place at [[Toprawa/Legends|Toprawa]] with the operation identified as [[Operation Skyhook]].
*The Battle of [[Scarif]] included a [[Sphyrna-class Hammerhead corvette|''Sphyrna''-class Hammerhead corvette]], based on the [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] first seen in the Legends video game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''.
*The [[death trooper]]s deployed at Scarif are similar to the [[Shadowtrooper]]s from the Legends video game [[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast|''Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast'']].

==[[Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VIII ''The Last Jedi'']]==
*Luke Skywalker during his exile on [[Ahch-To]] [[Sever Force|cuts himself from the Force]]. This ability was introduced in Legends and was used by [[Meetra Surik]], [[Kyle Katarn]] and others.
*Luke tricks his nephew [[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren]] by using a [[Force projection]] through the Force to allow the [[Resistance]] time to escape. This is similar to how he used projections of himself to confuse his nephew [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo/Darth Caedus]] into thinking he was attacking him instead of his sister [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina]].
*The organization of four units of two in the [[Praetorian Guard|Elite Praetorian Guard]] that guard [[Snoke]] may be loosely based on the [[Four unidentified Imperial Royal Guards|four Imperial Royal Guards]] that impersonated [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] in an attempt to lead an Imperial splinter faction known as the [[Second Imperium]]. Likewise, the [[First Order]] was originally formed by a splinter faction of the [[Galactic Empire]].
*Luke Skywalker dies and becomes one with the Force. In the Legends continuity, however, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke's]] passing happened offscreen.

==''[[Solo: A Star Wars Story]]''==
*Han Solo gets enrolled at the [[Carida Academy/Legends|Academy of Carida]] where he gets criticized for conduct reports. In the Legends continuity, however, Solo graduated at the top of his class and wasn't kicked out.
*Both [[Lady]] [[Proxima]] and [[Tobias Beckett]] are similar to [[Garris Shrike]], a main character in ''[[The Han Solo Trilogy]]'', who served as Han Solo's mentor in the Legends continuity. Likewise, [[Qi'ra]] is similar to [[Bria Tharen]], Han Solo's first love in Legends.
*Qi'ra uses [[Teräs Käsi]], a martial art that was introduced in the Legends ''Shadows of the Empire'' novel and was most notably featured in ''[[Star Wars: Masters of Teräs Käsi]]''.
*Han Solo releases Chewbacca from Imperial imprisonment, leading the Wookiee to swear a [[life debt]].
*[[Mimban]], a planet that appears in ''[[Splinter of the Mind's Eye]]'', shows up in the film.
*[[Dryden Vos]]'s [[Dryden's study|study]] is filled with several artifacts with references to Legends stories, such as the [[Masthead of Xim the Despot]] as a reference to the cover of ''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]''. Additionally, the [[Jewel of Yavin/Legends|Jewel of Yavin]] was initially intended to appear, but was dropped in production. Also, the ''[[Calrissian Chronicles]]'' trilogy is a reference to ''[[The Lando Calrissian Adventures]]'' book series.
*[[L3-37]], [[Landonis Balthazar Calrissian|Lando Calrissian's]] [[droid]] companion, is similar to [[Vuffi Raa]] from ''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'', who also served as the co-pilot of the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']].
*[[Maw Cluster/Legends|The Maw]] first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: The Jedi Academy Trilogy]]''. In ''[[The Han Solo Trilogy]]'', the Maw distorts space and time near [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]], so the distance of the [[Kessel Run/Legends|Kessel Run]] is shortened by flying close to it. The ''Falcon'' makes the shortcut while escaping from an Imperial customs ship.

==[[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''Star Wars'': Episode IX ''The Rise of Skywalker'']]==
*It's not known whether this was a coincidence or not, but the story of [[Darth Sidious]]' resurrection after his first death during the [[Battle of Endor]] seems to borrow some elements from the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series:
**After resurfacing from the dead, Sidious organizes a [[Final Order|Sith fleet]] to further his galactic conquest when time comes. This seems to have been inspired by the [[Katana fleet]], which is also comprised of a massive amount of Imperial ships stored away in a far off corner of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].
**Another possible nod to the story is that [[Supreme Leader]] [[Snoke]] is shown to have been an artificial [[humanoid]] being who had many [[Cloning|clone]] bodies stored by Sidious, similarly to how Sidious had many [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] bodies to sustain himself so he could rule the galaxy in the comic book. Perhaps as a reference to the comic series; the concept of cloning his original body, later confirmed in the novelization, is briefly brought up for Palpatine's return.
** The [[Sith Eternal]] and the [[Final Order]], two organizations lead by Sidious to reconquer [[the galaxy]] are similar to the [[Dark Empire]], a regime which Palpatine intended to use to reconquer the galaxy. Likewise, the Dark Empire shows Sidious no longer hiding his affiliation with the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]].
**The [[Knights of Ren]], a group of dark-side users which serve the resurrected Darth Sidious, are similar to the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] body called the [[Dark Side Elite]], founded by the reborn Sidious as part of the Dark Empire. Similarly, the Dark Side Elite and the Knights of Ren serve Sidious' new regime and consist of seven members each.
**The Sith [[Sovereign Protector]]s which serve as Darth Sidious' new royal guard appear to be at least partly based off of the [[Imperial Sovereign Protector]]s of the Royal Guard.
**The [[Sith Eternal]] cult's role in the [[Final Order]] mirrors that of the role the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]] cult had in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], as does their role as servants of Darth Sidious.
**[[Exegol]], the capital of the Sith Eternal/Final Order in the [[Unknown Regions]] resembles [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] which served as the seat of power for the Dark Empire complete with both planets having a blue hue and strong in the Dark side of the Force.
**In both cases, Sidious claimed ultimate authority over the entire dark side only to see his own powers fatally redirected against him by opponent. In the ''Dark Empire'', Sidious claimed himself to be the dark side itself and spawned a [[Force storm (wormhole)|Force storm]], but Luke and Leia used [[Force harmony]] to sever his Force connection, which made the storm rogue and it turned against [[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|Sidious' own flagship]], resulting in death of Sidious' current clone body. In ''The Rise of Skywalker'', Sidious claimed himself to be all the Sith shortly before Rey redirected his own massive torrent of Force lightning back to the Dark Lord, to the same effect.
*Besides ''Dark Empire'', the Final Order/Sith Eternal including [[Sith Eternal military|their military]], the Knights of Ren and their home planet of Exegol resemble the [[Eternal Empire]] from ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' including their [[Eternal Empire military|own military]], the [[Knights of Zakuul]] and their homeworld of [[Zakuul]]. Both empires are also based in the [[Unknown Regions]] and ruled by a Sith Emperor (Sheev Palpatine/Darth Sidious and [[Tenebrae|Vitiate/Valkorion]]) who can transfer their conscience from one body to another and launched their attempted conquests on the galaxy during the later stages of a major conflict ([[Galactic War]] and the [[First Order-Resistance War]]).
*The [[Xyston-class Star Destroyer|''Xyston''-class Star Destroyers]] of the Sith Eternal fleet, armed with planet-destroying [[axial superlaser]]s, bear some resemblance to the Legends' [[Conqueror (superlaser)|''Conqueror'']], an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] armed with superlaser purpoted to destroy planets.
*[[C-3PO]] questioning whether there's an afterlife for [[droid]]s might be a reference to his philosophical story arc in the ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order]]'' novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]''.
*Although it was mentioned in the novel ''[[Aftermath: Life Debt]]'' that Emperor Palpatine had no known family, the film reveals that Rey belongs to the [[Palpatine family]], from which the only survivors are Palpatine himself and Rey. In the novel [[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']], [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]] manipulated Palpatine into killing his [[House Palpatine/Legends|family]].
*Emperor Palpatine is revealed to have sired a son named [[Dathan]]. In the Legends continuity, Palpatine was the father of [[Triclops]], a [[mutant]] [[Slavery|slave]] who was supposedly the result of Palpatine's relationship with [[Sly Moore/Legends|Sly Moore]].
**Likewise, both canon and Legends contiunities involve Sidious' grandchild becoming a Jedi apprentice of Luke Skywalker. In the [[Adventures of a Jedi Prince|''Jedi Prince'']] series [[Ken]], son of Triclops, became an apprentice to Luke Skywalker, and was long unaware of his lineage. Much like Rey, he was terrified of it when learning the truth, and so deemed himself born of evil and thus unworthy to join the Rebel Alliance and in ''The Rise of Skywalker'' Rey deemed herself unworthy to be the Jedi, but Ken in Legends was reassured by Luke, who told him that he could be a force for good and likewise in ''The Rise of Skywalker'' Luke reassured Rey that some things are stronger than blood relations.
*While not known if an homage or a coincidence, the appearance of "Empress Palpatine" (which Rey imagines as herself if she falls to the [[dark side of the Force]]) resembles that of [[Eternal Empire|Eternal]] Empress [[Vaylin]] from ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''.
*Sidious inciting Rey to strike him down so he can [[Transference|transfer his spirit into her]] and rule together may be a reference to the [[transfer essence]] power which was used, or intended to be used successfully, by multiple [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] in the Legends continuity, including [[Emperor/Legends|Emperors]] Palpatine and Vitiate/Valkorion.
**Likewise, Sidious' plot to take over Rey may have been inspired by [[Cronal]]'s scheme in the novel ''[[Luke Skywalker and the Shadows of Mindor]]'', where Cronal intended to do the same to Luke Skywalker, aside that the [[Sith Citadel throne room]] shares a heavy resemblance to Cronal's [[Shadow Throne]].
**Sidious' ostentible promise to Rey, that after killing him she would be in charge of his immense fleet and, thereby, able to save her friends from the imminent destruction, might be inspired by the closing days of the Legends' continuity [[war against Zakuul]], in which [[Alliance Commander|the Commander]] of the [[Eternal Alliance]] had to take the [[Eternal Throne]] to pacify the [[Eternal Fleet]] and thereby stop its [[Assault on Odessen#Aftermath|reactivated]] original program of exterminating everything in the galaxy. In both cases, implicit posession-related Sith trap was present: had Rey completed the ritual her body would be seized by Darth Sidious, and the Alliance Commander's body was almost seized by the Emperor Valkorion, who attempted to wipe the Commander's consciousness from it immediately after the Commander sat onto the Eternal Throne.
*[[Landonis Balthazar Calrissian|Lando Calrissian]] summoning a massive fleet of civilian volunteers with the [[Citizens' Fleet]] is similar to the [[emergency evac fleet]] that assisted the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]] in the [[Legacy (2006) 47|47th issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' comic series. Like the citizen's fleet, the emergency evac fleet was made up of several vehicles from previous ''Star Wars'' stories and opposed a [[Genocide on Dac|genocide]] led by [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Sith-Imperial forces]].
*Rey's confrontation against Darth Sidious bears some resemblance to Sidious' [[Battle on Death Star I|confrontation]] against [[Galen Marek]] in the ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'' video game, as both confrontations involved the use of Force lightning.
